chunks.text:
# Manufacturing Business Glossary

This glossary defines the core business concepts and terminology used in the manufacturing supply chain domain. It covers product structures, materials management, production workflows, and quality assurance processes.

## Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex product... [TRUNCATED]

## Warehouse

A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique warehouse identifier
- Warehouse name and location
- Address, city, and ... [TRUNCATED]

## QualityControl

Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods. QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique QC identifier
- Batch reference (pr... [TRUNCATED]

# Manufacturing Data Dictionary

This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system. It includes all tables, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships. Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures.

## product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key.

| Column | Da... [TRUNCATED]

## bom

Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure. Supports recursive relationships for multi-level assemblies.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| bom_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique BOM identifier |
| parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) |
| component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → produc... [TRUNCATED]

## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier |
| warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name |
| address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |
| city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |
| state | VARCHAR(50) | | State or province |
| capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CH... [TRUNCATED]

## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |
| product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |
| parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition... [TRUNCATED]

## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique specification identifier |
| specification_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Specification name |
| version | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL | Specification version |
| effective_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Effective date |
| spec_type | spec_type_t | ... [TRUNCATED]

## shipment

Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| shipment_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique shipment identifier |
| shipment_type | shipment_type_t | NOT NULL | INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL |
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse reference |
| supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | S... [TRUNCATED]

entities_post_resolution.name: Bill Of Materials (Bom)
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Bill of Materials (BOM) is a comprehensive list that specifies the components, sub-assemblies, and quantities required to manufacture a product, often organized hierarchically.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: The Bill of Materials (BOM) defines the composition of products by specifying which components and sub-assemblies are required to manufacture a parent product. | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | BOM connects Products to other Products (parent-child) | BOM levels support hierarchical product explosions | BOM structure is used for material requirements planning | Multiple BOM entries define a complete product structure

entities_post_resolution.name: Version Numbers Effective
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Product in the business domain represents any manufactured item, including raw materials, components, assemblies, and finished goods, that are produced, stored, and managed within the manufacturing system.
synonyms: Stores physical storage location information for inventory management
a parent product, a component product, the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level
a production job or manufacturing run
a specific production lot or run of a product, tracking all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle
a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production
all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components
an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to the manufacturing organization
any manufactured item in the system
atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers
atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items
components they can provide through approved supplier relationships
hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders
inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods
manufactured products meet engineering and customer requirements
stock levels for components and products at specific warehouse locations
technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products
the actual stock levels of components and products stored in specific warehouses
the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system
the physical transfer of materials between warehouses, from suppliers to warehouses (inbound), or from warehouses to customers (outbound)
the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status
the sequence of manufacturing operations or steps required to produce a product
the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Specifications include version numbers, effective dates, specification types (dimensional, material, performance), critical parameters to test, and acceptable value ranges with units of measure. | A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items. | A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy) | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | A Supplier is an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to the manufacturing organization. | | component_category | VARCHAR(50) | | Component category classification | | | component_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Component name | | Components are supplied by multiple Suppliers (many-to-many) | Components have Inventory

entities_post_resolution.name: Active Status Flag
entities_post_resolution.definition: An attribute that indicates whether a record or entity is currently active or inactive within a system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | is_active | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'Y' | Active status flag |

entities_post_resolution.name: Standard Procurement Cost
entities_post_resolution.definition: Base manufacturing cost is the direct cost associated with producing a product, excluding overheads and profit margins.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost | | | base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost |

entities_post_resolution.name: Production Batch
entities_post_resolution.definition: A production batch is a specific group of units produced together during a manufacturing cycle, used for tracking, quality control, and inventory management.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Batch represents a specific production lot or run of a product, tracking all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle. | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches enable traceability for quality issues and inventory management for time-sensitive or lot-controlled products. | QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

entities_post_resolution.name: Qc Activities
entities_post_resolution.definition: QC activities encompass all tasks and procedures involved in quality control processes, including testing, inspection, and monitoring, while QC records are the documented evidence and data resulting from these activities.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested. | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.name: Manufacturing Route
entities_post_resolution.definition: A manufacturing route is a defined sequence of operations and steps required to produce a product, including individual operation details and the overall workflow.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Route defines the sequence of manufacturing operations or steps required to produce a product. | Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number. | Routes collectively define the manufacturing workflow and are used for production scheduling, capacity planning, and cost calculation.

entities_post_resolution.name: Ea Kg M L
entities_post_resolution.definition: An entity representing the measurement units used to quantify items, such as each (EA), kilogram (KG), meter (M), and liter (L).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.) |

entities_post_resolution.name: Capacity
entities_post_resolution.definition: Capacity refers to the maximum storage or load that a system or container can hold.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK > 0 | Storage capacity | | | city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location | | | state | VARCHAR(50) | | State or province |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity Reserved
entities_post_resolution.definition: quantity_on_hand: the amount of stock currently available for use or sale; quantity_reserved: the amount of stock reserved and not available for general use.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_reserved | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Reserved quantity | | | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quality Control (Qc)
entities_post_resolution.definition: Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods.

entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system.

entities_post_resolution.definition: Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers.

entities_post_resolution.definition: BOM levels support hierarchical product explosions
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: BOM levels support hierarchical product explosions

entities_post_resolution.definition: BOM structure is used for material requirements planning
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: BOM structure is used for material requirements planning

entities_post_resolution.definition: Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

entities_post_resolution.definition: | reorder_threshold | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00 | Reorder trigger level |

entities_post_resolution.name: Operation Name Work
entities_post_resolution.definition: Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number.

entities_post_resolution.name: Traceability For Quality
entities_post_resolution.definition: Batches enable traceability for quality issues and inventory management for time-sensitive or lot-controlled products.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Batches enable traceability for quality issues and inventory management for time-sensitive or lot-controlled products.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about products, including their identifiers, names, types, hierarchical relationships, costs, lead times, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Component
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about various components used in manufacturing or assembly processes, including their identifiers, names, categories, measurement units, costs, and related specifications... [TRUNCATED]

tables_enriched.name: Bill of Materials
tables_enriched.description: This table stores the components and structure of a product's bill of materials, detailing which components are used in each product and their quantities.

tables_enriched.name: Supplier
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about suppliers providing goods or services to the company, including contact details and preference status.

tables_enriched.name: Warehouse
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about warehouse locations, their capacities, and management details.

tables_enriched.name: Inventory
tables_enriched.description: This table tracks inventory levels and restocking information for products stored across various warehouses.

tables_enriched.name: Work Order
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about manufacturing or maintenance work orders, including their status, quantities, scheduling, and associated warehouse details.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: This table records details about shipments, including their types, associated warehouses, suppliers, customers, and key dates related to shipment scheduling and delivery status.

tables_enriched.name: Quality Control
tables_enriched.description: This table stores records of quality control inspections, including details about batch testing, inspection results, and defect counts to ensure product quality standards.

tables_enriched.name: Specification
tables_enriched.description: This table stores specifications details, including versioning, effective dates, and critical parameters for product or process standards.

tables_enriched.name: Batch
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about production batches of products, including production details, quantities, storage locations, and quality control status.

tables_enriched.name: Route
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about manufacturing or process routes, including the sequence of operations, associated work centers, and timing details for each route.

tables_enriched.name: Component Supplier
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about the suppliers for each component, including preference status, lead time, and pricing details.
